stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (the established treatment) yields a guaranteed return of $108K. Option B (the experimental treatment) depends on patient response rate: with probability 35-56% (uncertain), it yields $193K; otherwise it yields $15K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in patient response rate affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $108K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $77.3K to $114.7K. Point estimate: $96.9K. Option A is preferred at most assumptions (EV_A > EV_B when P(good) < 52.2%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 52.2%, which falls within the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
